Event: Hamas Attack on Israel
Description: Hamas launched a coordinated surprise offensive on Israel with 3000+ rockets and ~2500 militants breaching the Gaza-Israel barrier. Iran-backed groups celebrated the attacks.
Impact/Outcome: Sparked the broader Middle East crisis; Iran's Axis of Resistance activated

Event: Iran-backed Militia Attacks Begin
Description: Iran-backed proxy forces ramped up strikes on US and Israeli targets in Iraq and Syria; Hezbollah opened a northern Lebanon front; over 200 attacks in weeks.
Impact/Outcome: 170+ attacks on US military assets in Iraq, Syria, and Jordan between Oct 2023 – Feb 2024

Event: US Airstrikes on Iran-backed Facilities
Description: US ordered airstrikes on two Iran-backed facilities in retaliation for proxy attacks on US personnel and assets.
Impact/Outcome: US signals willingness to hit Iranian proxies directly

Event: Houthi Red Sea Campaign Begins
Description: Houthis began targeting international shipping lanes and Israeli-linked vessels in the Red Sea in solidarity with Gaza, escalating global trade disruption.
Impact/Outcome: Major shipping disruption; triggered US-led naval coalition Operation Prosperity Guardian

Event: Jordan Tower 22 Attack
Description: Iran-backed militias struck a US military outpost in Jordan (Tower 22), killing 3 US service members and injuring dozens.
Impact/Outcome: First US fatalities from proxy attacks; major escalation point

Event: US Retaliatory Strikes – Operation Inherent Resolve
Description: US launched airstrikes on 85 Iran-affiliated targets across Iraq and Syria in response to the Tower 22 attack.
Impact/Outcome: Largest US retaliation against Iran-backed groups since 2003

Event: Israel Strikes Iranian Consulate in Damascus
Description: Israel bombed the Iranian consulate complex in Damascus, killing multiple senior Iranian Revolutionary Guard officers.
Impact/Outcome: Iran vowed retaliation; major direct escalation

Event: Iran Launches First Direct Strike on Israel
Description: Iran and its allies launched 300+ drones and ballistic missiles directly at Israel. Israeli defenses (with US support) intercepted most projectiles.
Impact/Outcome: First ever direct Iranian strike on Israel; US directly helped Israel defend

Event: Israel Retaliatory Strike on Iran
Description: Israel launched limited retaliatory strikes targeting Iranian air defenses. Analysts described it as signaling a desire to de-escalate.
Impact/Outcome: Tensions cooled temporarily; conflict remained at proxy level for months

Event: Iran Fires 180 Ballistic Missiles at Israel
Description: Following Israeli assassinations of Hamas and Hezbollah leaders, Iran launched 180 ballistic missiles against Israel.
Impact/Outcome: Major escalation; Israel vowed and carried out strikes on Iranian air defenses and missile production facilities

Event: Israel Strikes Iranian Air Defenses & Missile Sites
Description: Israel conducted its largest-ever direct attack on Iran, targeting air defense systems and missile production facilities.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's air defenses significantly degraded; Hezbollah and Hamas leadership decimated

Event: Assad Regime Falls in Syria
Description: The Bashar al-Assad regime collapsed, depriving Iran of a critical land corridor and strategic ally in its Axis of Resistance.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's regional influence severely weakened; proxy network fragmented

Event: Trump Returns – Maximum Pressure Resumed
Description: President Trump restored maximum pressure economic sanctions campaign against Iran upon returning to office. Also initiated first direct US-Iran nuclear talks since 2018.
Impact/Outcome: Dual track of pressure + diplomacy; Iran's economy further strained

Event: Twelve-Day War Begins – Israel Strikes Iran
Description: Israel launched a surprise attack targeting key Iranian military and nuclear facilities; included targeted assassinations of Iranian military leaders and nuclear scientists.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's defenses and nuclear program significantly damaged

Event: Iran and Houthis Strike Israel Jointly
Description: Iran and the Houthis jointly struck Jaffa with long-range ballistic missiles — the first such joint Iran-militia strike directly inside Israel.
Impact/Outcome: Marked major escalation; US joined Israeli air operations

Event: US Airstrikes on Iranian Nuclear Sites
Description: The US conducted airstrikes aimed at destroying Iran's nuclear facilities during the Twelve-Day War, effectively joining Israel's military campaign.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's nuclear program severely disrupted; 12-day war ended in ceasefire

Event: Iran Protests Escalate
Description: Largest protests in Iran since 1979 erupted over a weakened economy and crumbling infrastructure; Iranian security forces responded with extreme violence.
Impact/Outcome: Regime's domestic legitimacy severely undermined

Event: Iran Massacres Civilian Protesters
Description: Iranian security forces massacred thousands of civilians in cracking down on mass protests, drawing global condemnation.
Impact/Outcome: Trump threatened military action; US began largest regional military buildup since 2003 Iraq invasion

Event: Trump Calls for Regime Change in Iran
Description: Trump publicly stated regime change in Iran would be 'the best thing that could happen'. A day later US officials confirmed preparations for sustained military operations.
Impact/Outcome: Set stage for military action; Iran alerted to imminent threat

Event: Trump State of the Union – Iran Nuclear Warning
Description: Trump accused Iran of reviving nuclear weapons development and advancing missile capabilities threatening Europe and US bases. Warned the US was prepared to act.
Impact/Outcome: Diplomatic ultimatum effectively issued to Iran

Event: Iran Claims Historic Nuclear Deal 'Within Reach'
Description: Iranian FM Araghchi stated a 'historic' agreement to avert military conflict was within reach ahead of renewed talks in Geneva. Iran reiterated opposition to nuclear weapons.
Impact/Outcome: Diplomatic window opened; ultimately failed to prevent strikes

Event: Operation Epic Fury – US & Israel Strike Iran
Description: US and Israel launched nearly 900 strikes in 12 hours targeting Iranian missiles, air defenses, military infrastructure, and leadership. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was assassinated. Strikes occurred during active negotiations.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's supreme leader killed; largest US-Israel joint operation in history

Event: Iran Retaliates – Missile and Drone Strikes
Location: Israel / US military bases / Arab Gulf States
Description: Iran responded to US-Israeli strikes with massive missile and drone barrages against Israel, US bases across the Middle East, and US-allied Gulf Arab states.
Impact/Outcome: Widespread regional destabilization; GCC states targeted

Event: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
Description: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil supply passes, disrupting global trade and energy markets.
Impact/Outcome: Global oil prices surged; US threatened 'extensive attacks' on Iranian energy infrastructure if not reopened by April 6

Event: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed New Supreme Leader
Description: Following Ali Khamenei's assassination, Mojtaba Khamenei (his son) was selected as the new Supreme Leader of Iran.
Impact/Outcome: Iran's political continuity maintained; regime did not collapse

Event: Iran Protests Regime – Internal Pressure Mounts
Description: Widespread protests erupted inside Iran, motivated by economic collapse and weakened infrastructure following the strikes. Security forces used extensive force to suppress protests.
Impact/Outcome: Regime faced deepest domestic legitimacy crisis in its history

Event: Trump Offers Talks – Sanctions-for-Enrichment Deal
Description: Trump stated openness to talks: Iran ending all nuclear enrichment in exchange for full US sanctions relief. Iran denied formal talks while reportedly having conditions.
Impact/Outcome: Conflict ongoing; ceasefire negotiations uncertain as of April 2026

Event: US Ultimatum – Reopen Strait of Hormuz
Description: Trump threatened extensive attacks on Iranian energy sites if Iran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by April 6 2026. More US troops ordered into region.
Impact/Outcome: Strait status unresolved as of early April 2026; situation remains volatile
